name: Jolt Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Line the bottom of a buttered 9-inch cake pan, 2-inches deep, with parchment paper and butter the paper.
2. In a small saucepan, melt the butter over moderately low heat. Stir in the espresso, sugar, orange zest, orange extract, and Grand Marnier until combined.
3. In the top of a double boiler set over barely simmering water, melt the semisweet and unsweetened chocolate, stirring occasionally.
4. Stir the melted chocolates into the butter mixture, then add the eggs, stirring until just combined.
5. Pour the mixture into the prepared pan.
6. Place the pan in a baking dish and add enough hot water to the dish to reach halfway up the side of the pan.
7. Bake in the center of a preheated 350°F oven for 1 hour.
8. Remove the cake from the water bath, let it cool on a rack, and chill, covered loosely, for 3 hours.
9. Run a thin knife around the inside of the pan and dip the bottom of the pan in hot water for 3 seconds.
10. Invert a platter over the pan, invert the cake onto the platter with a sharp rap, and cut with a sharp knife dipped in hot water, cleaning the knife before cutting each slice.
